message:
feat(index): lexical rebuild progress reporting, daily_stats drift repair, and status refactor

Lexical rebuild improvements (src/indexer/mod.rs):
- Add count_total_conversations_exact() that uses COUNT(*) FROM conversations
  instead of count_sessions_in_range() which does range-based filtering —
  avoids miscounts when the range parameters interact with session boundaries
- Remove total_conversations parameter from lexical_rebuild_db_state() since
  it now queries the count internally, eliminating 5 callsites that each had
  to compute the count independently with different methods
- Add clear_lexical_rebuild_state() to remove stale rebuild snapshots when
  running --full without a pending rebuild (prevents false resume on next run)
- Add repair_daily_stats_if_drifted() that detects when the daily_stats
  materialized view has drifted from canonical conversations (via
  daily_stats_health()) and rebuilds it before index planning — fixes a class
  of bugs where stale stats caused incorrect session selection during rebuild
- Call repair_daily_stats_if_drifted() both before initial scan (--full) and
  after historical salvage imports new conversations
- Add LexicalRebuildSnapshot struct and load_lexical_rebuild_snapshot() to
  expose in-progress rebuild state (processed/total conversations, indexed
  docs, timestamps) to status commands
- Gate reset_storage() behind #[cfg(test)] — it was only used in tests but
  was compiled into release builds
- Add test: repair_daily_stats_if_drifted_rebuilds_materialized_totals
- Add test: clear_lexical_rebuild_state_removes_stale_snapshot

Status command refactor (src/lib.rs):
- Refactor run_status() to delegate entirely to state_meta_json() instead of
  duplicating database probing, index age calculation, and staleness logic —
  the structured JSON output is now derived from the same source as the
  --robot-meta output, ensuring consistency
- Add IndexRunSnapshot and probe_index_run_lock() that reads the
  index-run.lock file, checks whether the holding PID still has an exclusive
  flock, and returns active/pid/started_at — enables detecting in-progress
  rebuilds without polling the process table
- Add "rebuild" section to state_meta_json() and status output with active,
  pid, started_at, updated_at, processed_conversations, total_conversations,
  indexed_docs fields
- Add "rebuilding" and "activity_at" fields to the index section
- Status health now considers rebuild_active: a rebuilding index is not
  "healthy" and gets the "rebuilding" status string with appropriate
  recommended_action
- Conditionally include conversation/message counts only for databases under
  STATUS_COUNT_SCAN_MAX_DB_BYTES (was previously always skipping counts in
  state_meta_json but always including them in status)
- Add "rebuilding" field to state_index_freshness() helper
- Add fs2 crate import for flock-based lock probing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
